recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil pork ears and snout until tender.
2. Grill until crispy.
3. Chop into small pieces.
4. Sauté onions and chili peppers.
5. Add chopped pork, calamansi, and seasonings.
6. Serve on sizzling plate with egg on top.

1. Combine meat, soy sauce, vinegar, crushed garlic, bay leaf, and peppercorns. Marinate for 30 minutes.
2. Transfer to a pot and bring to a boil. Do not stir.
3. Lower the heat and simmer until meat is tender and the sauce is reduced.
4. Serve hot with rice.

1. Boil pork ribs until tender, skimming off impurities.
2. Add tomatoes, onions, and radish.
3. Stir in sinigang tamarind powder mix.
4. Add long beans, eggplant, and kangkong (water spinach).
5. Season with fish sauce and green chili (siling haba).
6. Simmer until vegetables are cooked. Serve hot.

1. Cook thick noodles and set aside.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and annatto in oil. Add shrimp broth, ground pork, and thickened with cornstarch.
3. Pour the sauce over the noodles.
4. Garnish with smoked fish flakes (tinapa), crushed chicharon, boiled shrimp, hard-boiled egg, and green onions.

1. Boil beef or pork until tender, skimming off impurities.
2. Add peppercorns and onion to the broth.
3. Add potatoes and cook until half-done.
4. Add cabbage and bok choy (pechay). Cook until tender.
5. Season with salt. Serve hot as a simple, clear soup.

1. Boil whole pork leg (pata) with spices (bay leaf, peppercorn, salt) until very tender.
2. Drain the pata and let it air dry completely for several hours (or overnight in the fridge).
3. Deep fry the dried pata in hot oil until the skin is golden brown and extremely crispy.
4. Chop into serving pieces and serve with a vinegar-soy dipping sauce.

1. Boil pork belly until tender, skimming off impurities.
2. Rub the pork skin generously with salt and pepper. Air dry completely.
3. Deep fry the pork belly in hot oil until the skin is crispy and blistered.
4. Chop into cubes and serve with a dipping sauce like Mang Tomas or a vinegar-soy mix.

1. Clean a whole fish (e.g., Tilapia or Lapu-Lapu).
2. Stuff the cavity with sliced tomatoes, onions, ginger, and lemon/calamansi grass.
3. Wrap the stuffed fish tightly in banana leaves or aluminum foil.
4. Grill or bake until cooked through.
5. Serve with soy sauce-calamansi dipping sauce.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned.
3. Pour in coconut milk and water. Bring to a boil.
4. Add squash and green beans. Simmer until the chicken and vegetables are tender.
5. Season with salt/fish sauce and a little sugar to balance the flavor.
6. Serve hot with rice.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and chicken/pork until browned.
2. Add tomato sauce, water, and bay leaf. Simmer until meat is tender.
3. Add potatoes and carrots, cook until half-done.
4. Add bell peppers and green peas. Simmer until vegetables are tender.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot with rice.

1. Brown cubed beef in oil and set aside.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in the same pot. Add tomato sauce and water.
3. Return the beef, add soy sauce, bay leaves, and peppercorns. Simmer until meat is tender.
4. Add potatoes and cook until half-done.
5. Add carrots and cook until all vegetables are tender.
6. Season with salt. Serve hot with rice.

1. Boil beef until tender, skimming off impurities.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in oil. Add the tender beef and sauté.
3. Add tomatoes, tomato sauce, and beef broth.
4. Add potatoes, saba banana (plantain), and garbanzo beans. Simmer until tender.
5. Add pechay (bok choy) and green beans right before serving.
6. Serve with a dipping sauce of mashed eggplant, vinegar, and garlic.

1. Boil pork belly until tender, skimming off impurities.
2. Add kadyos (pigeon peas) and unripe langka (jackfruit). Simmer until soft.
3. Add batuan (sour fruit) or tamarind mix for sourness.
4. Add long green chilies (siling haba) and season with salt/fish sauce.
5. Simmer until the dish achieves the desired sour and rich flavor. Serve hot.

1. Marinate chicken breast/thighs in soy sauce, calamansi juice, and pepper for 30 minutes.
2. Pan-fry the chicken quickly and set aside.
3. Fry onion rings until lightly browned and set aside.
4. Pour the leftover marinade into the pan. Add a little water/broth and simmer.
5. Return the chicken to the sauce and simmer until the chicken is cooked and the sauce is reduced.
6. Garnish with fried onion rings and serve with rice.

1. Arrange sliced fish (e.g., Tilapia, Galunggong) in a pot with sliced ginger, garlic, and onions.
2. Add vinegar, water, salt, and whole peppercorns.
3. Add long green chili (siling haba) and bitter gourd (ampalaya) slices (optional).
4. Bring to a boil, then simmer without stirring until the fish is cooked and the liquid is reduced.
5. Serve hot with rice.

1. Marinate pork ribs with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Pan-fry until browned.
2. Set aside the ribs. In the same pan, sauté mushrooms.
3. Add flour, then pour in beef/pork broth and cream.
4. Stir until the gravy thickens. Season with soy sauce, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce.
5. Return the ribs to the gravy and simmer for 5-10 minutes. Serve hot with rice.

1. Marinate pork belly in soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, and peppercorns.
2. Brown the pork belly in a pot.
3. Add the marinade, water, brown sugar, fermented black beans (tausi), and banana blossoms.
4. Simmer on low heat until the pork is fork-tender and the sauce is thick and rich.
5. Serve hot with rice.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add the cleaned tilapia and pour in coconut milk.
3. Add chili peppers (siling labuyo) and season with salt/fish sauce.
4. Bring to a simmer and cook until the fish is done and the sauce has thickened.
5. Optional: add a handful of malunggay (moringa) leaves before serving.

1. Marinate chicken pieces in a mixture of vinegar, calamansi juice, garlic, ginger, pepper, and lemon grass.
2. Grill the chicken over hot coals, basting frequently with an oil-annatto (achuete) mixture.
3. Cook until chicken is tender and has a nice char.
4. Serve with rice, soy sauce, and chili-calamansi dipping sauce.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and beef brisket/cubes until browned.
2. Add soy sauce, star anise, brown sugar, and water.
3. Simmer until the beef is fork-tender and the sauce has reduced to a thick, dark glaze.
4. Serve hot with garlic fried rice (sinangag) and a clear soup (pares soup).

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and various meats (pork, chicken, kikiam, liver) in oil.
2. Add chicken/pork broth and bring to a boil.
3. Add fresh egg noodles (lomi noodles) and simmer until cooked.
4. Thicken the soup with cornstarch slurry and beaten eggs.
5. Season with salt/fish sauce and serve hot, topped with fried garlic, spring onions, and calamansi.

1. Separate the meat from the bone of chicken wings to create a 'lollipop' shape.
2. Marinate the chicken in soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and egg.
3. Coat with a mixture of flour and cornstarch.
4. Deep fry until golden brown and crispy.
5. Serve with sweet and sour sauce or banana ketchup.

1. Prepare crispy pork belly (Lechon Kawali style). Chop into small pieces.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger. Add the pre-cooked Dinuguan (pork blood stew) base.
3. Simmer briefly until heated through.
4. Serve the Dinuguan sauce topped with the freshly chopped crispy pork belly.
5. Serve hot with Puto (steamed rice cake).

1. Prepare a flaky pie crust and line a pie dish.
2. Mix young coconut (buko) strips with condensed milk, coconut milk, cornstarch, and sugar.
3. Pour the mixture into the pie crust. Top with a lattice or solid second crust.
4. Bake until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
5. Cool completely before slicing and serving.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ground pork/shrimp in oil.
2. Add chili peppers and shrimp paste (bagoong) and cook briefly.
3. Add chopped wing beans (sigarilyas) or long beans (sitaw).
4. Pour in coconut milk and bring to a simmer.
5. Cook until the vegetables are tender and the sauce is reduced and creamy. Serve hot.

1. Cook the vegetable filling: sauté garlic and onion, add ground pork/shrimp, and various chopped vegetables (cabbage, carrots, green beans). Season with soy sauce/oyster sauce.
2. Prepare the wrapper: Cook a thin crepe from egg and flour.
3. Prepare the sauce: Boil water, brown sugar, soy sauce, and thicken with cornstarch. Add crushed garlic.
4. Assemble: Lay the wrapper flat, place a lettuce leaf, spoon the filling, and fold.
5. Pour the sauce over the roll and sprinkle with ground peanuts.

1. Boil stingray (pagi) in vinegar and water until tender. Flake the meat.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
3. Add the flaked stingray, sliced malunggay leaves (moringa), and chili peppers.
4. Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream. Bring to a simmer.
5. Cook until the sauce is thick and oily.
6. Season with salt and serve hot.

1. Clean and scale whole Tilapia. Rub with salt inside and out.
2. Score the sides of the fish.
3. Fry the Tilapia in hot oil until golden brown and crispy on both sides.
4. Drain excess oil and serve hot with a side of vinegar and garlic dipping sauce.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and chicken pieces until lightly browned.
2. Add water and simmer until the chicken is tender.
3. Add sliced tomatoes and green beans.
4. Whisk in a mixture of flour and milk/cream to thicken the sauce (sarsa).
5. Season with salt, pepper, and fish sauce. Serve hot.

1. Grill or roast whole eggplant until the skin is charred and the flesh is tender. Peel the skin.
2. Mash the eggplant and set aside.
3. Beat eggs and mix with garlic, onion, salt, and pepper. Optional: Add minced pork/beef.
4. Dip the mashed eggplant in the egg mixture.
5. Fry in a pan until the egg is cooked and golden brown.
6. Serve hot with banana ketchup.

1. Combine pork belly/hocks, vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, peppercorns, and bay leaves in a pot.
2. Add water and bring to a boil. Simmer until the pork is tender.
3. Add brown sugar and let the sauce reduce to a rich, tangy gravy.
4. Optional: Add a few slices of fried banana (saba) before serving.

1. Marinate chicken in pineapple juice and soy sauce for 30 minutes.
2. Sauté garlic and onion. Add the marinated chicken and cook until lightly browned.
3. Add pineapple chunks, milk/cream, and chicken broth.
4. Simmer until the chicken is tender and the sauce is thick and creamy.
5. Season with salt and pepper. Add sliced bell peppers before serving.

1. Slice cleaned squid into rings. Marinate briefly in calamansi juice, salt, and pepper.
2. Prepare the batter: Mix flour, cornstarch, salt, pepper, and a little baking powder with cold water.
3. Dip the squid rings into the batter.
4. Deep fry until golden brown and crispy.
5. Serve immediately with vinegar-garlic dipping sauce or mayonnaise.

1. Boil beef shanks with bone marrow and water for several hours until the meat is very tender.
2. Skim off impurities and season the broth with salt and peppercorns.
3. Add whole peppercorns, corn on the cob, and onion.
4. Add cabbage and bok choy (pechay) right before serving.
5. Serve piping hot with rice.

1. Boil pork lungs and heart until tender, then mince finely.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil. Add the minced meat and cook briefly.
3. Add water/broth and simmer.
4. Add diced carrots, bell peppers, and chili peppers.
5. Pour in vinegar and let it boil without stirring.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot.

1. Cook chicken adobo (or use leftover adobo). Shred the chicken meat.
2. In a pan, heat a little oil and sauté the shredded chicken until lightly crispy and dry.
3. Add some of the leftover adobo sauce/oil for flavor.
4. Serve hot with garlic fried rice (sinangag) and a fried egg (Adosilog).

1. Marinate tuna belly in soy sauce, calamansi juice, garlic, ginger, and pepper for 30 minutes.
2. Grill over medium-hot coals, brushing occasionally with the marinade.
3. Cook until the tuna is done but still moist.
4. Serve hot with dipping sauce (soy sauce, calamansi, chili) and rice.

1. Soak sliced bitter gourd (ampalaya) in salt water for 30 minutes to reduce bitterness. Rinse and drain.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes in oil. Add shrimp/ground pork (optional).
3. Add the bitter gourd slices and stir-fry briefly.
4. Whisk eggs, pour over the vegetables, and scramble lightly until cooked.
5. Season with salt/fish sauce and pepper. Serve hot.

1. Mix ground pork, minced shrimp, water chestnuts, carrots, sesame oil, soy sauce, salt, pepper, and egg.
2. Place a small amount of filling in the center of a siomai (wonton) wrapper. Gather the edges but leave the top open.
3. Steam the siomai for 15-20 minutes until cooked through.
4. Serve hot with a dipping sauce of soy sauce, calamansi, and chili garlic oil.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add chicken pieces and cook until brown.
3. Add rice and stir for a minute.
4. Pour in chicken broth and season with fish sauce. Bring to a boil, then simmer, stirring occasionally, until the rice is thick like porridge.
5. Serve hot, garnished with hard-boiled egg, fried garlic bits, spring onions, and calamansi.

1. Cook spaghetti noodles according to package directions.
2. Sauté garlic and onion. Add ground meat and hotdogs/sausages and cook until brown.
3. Pour in banana ketchup and tomato sauce. Add water or broth.
4. Add sugar and seasoning (salt, pepper). Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
5. Serve the sauce over the noodles, topped with shredded cheddar cheese.

1. Marinate pork with sugar, salt, and pineapple juice.
2. Add red food coloring if desired.
3. Marinate overnight in refrigerator.
4. Pan fry in low heat with a little water.
5. Cook until caramelized and slightly charred.
6. Serve with garlic fried rice and egg (Tosilog).

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and annatto seeds until fragrant.
2. Add oxtail and beef cuts, cook until browned.
3. Pour in beef broth and simmer until meat is tender.
4. Stir in peanut butter and ground toasted rice.
5. Add vegetables (banana blossoms, eggplant, string beans, pechay) and cook until tender-crisp.
6. Serve with shrimp paste (bagoong).

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned.
3. Add young tamarind leaves, tamarind broth, and water. Bring to a boil.
4. Add vegetables like green papaya or sayote and cook until tender.
5. Season with salt and fish sauce. Serve hot.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add pork slices and cook until brown.
3. Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream. Bring to a boil.
4. Add shrimp paste and lots of chili peppers. Simmer until the pork is tender and the sauce is thick and oily.
5. Serve hot with rice.

1. Clean the snails (kuhol) thoroughly and trim the ends.
2. Sauté garlic, ginger, and onion in oil.
3. Add the kuhol and mix well.
4. Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream. Add chili peppers.
5. Season with salt and fish sauce. Simmer until the sauce thickens and the snails are cooked.

1. Cook pancit canton noodles according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add chicken, pork, and shrimp, and cook until done.
4. Add vegetables (cabbage, carrots, snow peas) and cook for 2-3 minutes.
5. Pour in chicken broth, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Bring to a simmer.
6. Toss in the cooked noodles. Mix well until the sauce is absorbed. Serve with calamansi.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger.
2. Add pork belly and shrimp paste (bagoong) and cook until pork is browned.
3. Add squash and bitter gourd, simmer until slightly tender.
4. Add okra, eggplant, and string beans. Do not stir initially.
5. Cover and steam/simmer until vegetables are cooked but still crisp.
6. Season with water/broth and serve.

1. Slice pork belly into 1-inch thick pieces.
2. Marinate the pork in a mixture of soy sauce, vinegar, crushed garlic, pepper, and sugar for at least 4 hours or overnight.
3. Grill the pork belly over medium heat, brushing with the reserved marinade periodically.
4. Grill until the pork is fully cooked and slightly charred.
5. Serve with a dipping sauce of soy sauce, vinegar, chili, and onion.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and lots of ginger in oil.
2. Add chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned.
3. Pour in water and bring to a boil. Simmer until chicken is tender.
4. Add green papaya or sayote and cook until soft.
5. Stir in malunggay leaves (moringa) and season with fish sauce (patis) to taste. Serve hot.

1. Deep fry a whole cleaned fish (e.g., Tilapia or Lapu-Lapu) until golden brown and crispy. Set aside.
2. Sauté garlic, ginger, and onion.
3. Add julienned carrots, bell peppers, and stir-fry.
4. In a separate bowl, mix vinegar, sugar, water, soy sauce, and cornstarch for the sauce.
5. Pour the sauce mixture into the pan and cook until thick and glossy.
6. Pour the sauce over the fried fish and serve immediately.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in a pot.
2. Add the vegetables (e.g., string beans, squash, okra) and stir well.
3. Pour in coconut milk and season with salt and pepper.
4. Simmer until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened slightly.
5. Optional: Add shrimp or pork for extra flavor.

1. Prepare the Molo filling by mixing ground pork/shrimp with egg, seasonings, and minced water chestnuts.
2. Fill Molo wrappers with the mixture and seal.
3. In a pot, boil chicken broth.
4. Add chicken chunks and shrimp, and simmer.
5. Drop the Molo dumplings into the boiling broth and cook until they float.
6. Season with pepper, salt, and garnish with green onion and garlic bits.

1. Marinate pork belly in soy sauce, vinegar, and black pepper.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
3. Brown the marinated pork.
4. Add the marinade, bay leaf, sugar, and water. Simmer until the pork is tender.
5. Add fried saba bananas (plantains) and cook for a few minutes.
6. Add potatoes or carrots if desired. Serve hot.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and a generous amount of ginger in oil.
2. Add chicken pieces and cook until brown.
3. Add glutinous rice and mix well.
4. Pour in chicken broth and season with fish sauce and saffron/kasubha (for color).
5. Simmer, stirring occasionally, until the rice is cooked and the mixture is thick like porridge.
6. Serve hot, garnished with hard-boiled egg, fried garlic bits, green onions, and calamansi.

1. Marinate cubed pork in soy sauce and calamansi juice.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and add the marinated pork. Cook until brown.
3. Add tomato sauce and water. Simmer until the pork is tender.
4. Add potatoes, carrots, and cook until half-done.
5. Add cubed liver, raisins, and garbanzo beans. Simmer until all vegetables are tender.
6. Season with salt and pepper. Add green peas right before serving.

1. Boil pork bones to make a rich broth.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger, then add pork offal (liver, intestines) and shrimp.
3. Add the pork broth and bring to a boil.
4. Add miki noodles and simmer until cooked.
5. Season with patis (fish sauce), salt, and pepper.
6. Serve hot, garnished with chicharon (pork cracklings), hard-boiled egg slices, and spring onions.

1. Place leftover Lechon (roasted pig) pieces in a pot.
2. Add Lechon sauce, vinegar, soy sauce, bay leaves, whole peppercorns, and water.
3. Bring to a boil, then simmer on low heat until the meat is very tender and the sauce has reduced and thickened.
4. Season with sugar to balance the sourness and salt if needed.
5. Serve hot with rice.

1. Clean and boil pork leg (pata) until tender. Drain and set aside the broth.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and star anise.
3. Add the pork leg, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, mushroom, and the reserved broth.
4. Simmer on low heat until the sauce is reduced to a thick glaze and the meat is falling off the bone.
5. Thicken the sauce with cornstarch slurry if necessary. Garnish with pechay or broccoli.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add fresh shrimp and cook until they turn pink.
3. Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream. Add chili peppers.
4. Bring to a simmer and add kalabasa (squash) or string beans (optional).
5. Season with salt and fish sauce. Cook until the sauce is thick and the vegetables are tender.

1. Cook thick noodles (palabok or malabon noodles) according to package directions. Set aside.
2. Prepare the sauce: Sauté garlic, onion, and annatto in oil. Add the shrimp broth and thicken with cornstarch. Season with fish sauce.
3. Assemble: Place noodles on a plate. Pour the sauce over the noodles.
4. Garnish generously with boiled shrimp, smoked fish flakes (tinapa), crushed chicharon, boiled egg slices, and spring onions. Serve with calamansi.

1. Clean crabs (alimango). 
2. Sauté garlic, ginger, and onion in oil.
3. Add the crabs and pour in coconut milk and coconut cream. Add chili peppers.
4. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook until the crabs are done (shells turn red/orange) and the sauce has reduced and thickened.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Optional: add squash (kalabasa).

1. Sauté garlic, onion, ginger, and dried fish (dilis) in oil.
2. Add the dried taro leaves and pour in the coconut cream/milk.
3. Add pork cubes or shrimp (optional) and long green chilies.
4. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook without stirring until the leaves are tender and the sauce is thick and oily (laing na may gata).

1. Cut fresh, raw tuna into cubes. Rinse and drain completely.
2. 'Cook' the tuna by mixing it with white vinegar and a small amount of calamansi juice.
3. Add sliced red onions, ginger, and chili peppers.
4. Season generously with salt and pepper. Allow to 'cure' for 15-30 minutes before serving.
5. Optional: add a bit of coconut cream for a richer flavor (Kinilaw na may Gata).

1. Marinate beef/goat meat in vinegar, soy sauce, and calamansi juice. Brown in oil.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and add the marinated meat. Pour in tomato sauce and beef broth.
3. Add liver spread and peanut butter for a richer flavor. Simmer until meat is tender.
4. Add potatoes, carrots, and olives. Cook until vegetables are tender.
5. Add bell peppers before serving. Season with salt and pepper.

1. Boil mung beans (monggo) until soft and mushy. Set aside.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Add pork (usually fried or leftover lechon kawali) or shrimp and shrimp paste (bagoong).
4. Add the boiled mung beans and a little water/broth. Simmer.
5. Stir in bitter gourd leaves (talbos ng ampalaya) or malunggay leaves before serving.

1. Marinate beef sirloin in soy sauce, calamansi juice, and pepper for 30 minutes.
2. Pan-fry the beef quickly and set aside.
3. Fry onion rings until lightly browned and set aside.
4. Pour the leftover marinade into the pan. Add a little water/broth and simmer.
5. Return the beef to the sauce and simmer until the beef is cooked through and the sauce is reduced.
6. Garnish with fried onion rings and serve with rice.

1. Coat fish fillet (e.g., Cream Dory, Tilapia) in a batter and deep fry until crispy. Set aside.
2. Prepare the sauce: Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger.
3. Add pineapple chunks, bell peppers, carrots, water, sugar, and vinegar. Bring to a simmer.
4. Thicken the sauce with a cornstarch slurry. Season with salt.
5. Pour the sweet and sour sauce over the crispy fish fillet and serve hot.

1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger in oil.
2. Add the shrimp (sugpo) and cook until they turn pink.
3. Pour in coconut milk and a splash of water.
4. Add chili peppers and a few pieces of squash (kalabasa) or malunggay leaves (optional).
5. Simmer until the sauce thickens and the shrimp is fully cooked. Season with fish sauce/salt.

1. Marinate chicken pieces in a mixture of soy sauce, calamansi, garlic powder, and pepper.
2. Dredge the marinated chicken pieces in seasoned flour.
3. Deep fry the chicken in hot oil until golden brown and crispy on the outside, and fully cooked on the inside.
4. Serve hot with banana ketchup or gravy.

1. Mix ground pork, minced carrots, onion, egg, and seasonings (salt, pepper, soy sauce).
2. Lay a lumpia wrapper flat. Place a spoonful of the mixture near one edge.
3. Fold the sides in, then roll tightly into a thin cylinder. Seal the edge with water.
4. Deep fry until golden brown and crispy.
5. Serve with a sweet and sour dipping sauce.

1. Prepare Bagnet (Crispy Pork Belly, similar to Lechon Kawali) and cut into cubes.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and a generous amount of shrimp paste (bagoong) in oil.
3. Add squash and cook until half-done.
4. Add bitter gourd, okra, eggplant, and long beans. Add a little water/broth and cover (do not stir initially).
5. Simmer until the vegetables are cooked. Gently stir, then top with Bagnet cubes and serve hot.

1. In a tall glass/bowl, layer various sweet ingredients: sweet beans, coconut sport (macapuno), saba banana, jackfruit, sweet potato, kaong (sugar palm), and nata de coco.\n2. Fill the glass with shaved ice.\n3. Top the shaved ice with a generous pour of evaporated milk, a scoop of ube ice cream, a slice of leche flan, and toasted pinipig (pounded rice).

1. Deep-fry tofu until golden and crispy. Cube and set aside.
2. In a pan, sauté onion, ginger, and green chilies. Add chopped crispy tofu and seasonings (soy sauce, oyster sauce).
3. Stir in mayonnaise to create a creamy coating.
4. Serve on a sizzling plate, topped with egg (optional) and spring onions.

1. Marinate pork cubes in soy sauce and calamansi/lemon juice.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and add the marinated pork. Cook until browned.
3. Add tomato sauce, water, and simmer until the pork is half-tender.
4. Add potatoes, carrots, and cook until tender.
5. Stir in garbanzo beans (chickpeas) and sliced hotdogs or raisins (optional). Season with salt/sugar.
6. Serve hot with rice.

1. Boil water with tomatoes, onion, and radish.
2. Add shrimp and sinigang tamarind powder mix. Bring to a boil.
3. Add long beans (sitaw), eggplant, and okra.
4. Season with fish sauce and green chili (siling haba).
5. Simmer until the shrimp is cooked and the vegetables are tender. Serve hot.

1. Prepare the caramel by melting sugar directly in the llanera (oval tin molds). Let it cool and harden.
2. Whisk together egg yolks, condensed milk, evaporated milk, and vanilla extract.
3. Strain the mixture over the hardened caramel in the llanera.
4. Steam the flan for 30-45 minutes until set. Cool completely before chilling.
5. To serve, invert the llanera onto a plate to release the flan and the caramel sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. In pot over medium heat, heat oil. Add onions and garlic and cook, stirring regularly, until softened.
2. Add pork and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned.
3. Add shrimp paste and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until it begins to brown.
4. Add tomatoes and cook, mashing with the back of a spoon, until softened and have released juice.
5. Add water and bring to a boil. Lower heat, cover, and cook for about 15 to 20 minutes or until meat is tender. Add more water in ½ cup increments as needed to maintain about 1 cup of liquid.
6. Add squash and cook for about 2 minutes or until almost tender.
7. Add long beans and continue to cook until tender-crisp.
8. Add ampalaya, eggplant, and okra. Continue to cook for about 4 to 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender yet crisp.
9. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. In a pot over medium heat, combine water, onions, and garlic. Bring to a boil and cook until softened.
2. Add shrimp and calabasa. Cook for about 3 to 5 minutes or until shrimp changes color and squash is half done.
3. Add sitaw and cook for about 2 to 3 minutes.
4. Add eggplant, ampalaya, and okra, and cook for about 2 to 3 minutes.
5. Add patola and cook for another 1 to 2 minutes or until vegetables are tender yet crisp.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. In a pan over medium heat, heat oil. Add tilapia and fry, turning once or twice, until golden and cooked through. Remove from pan and drain on paper towels.
2. In a pot over medium heat, bring enough water to cover to a boil. Add labong and cook for about 20 to 25 minutes. Drain well and discard the liquid.
3. In another pot over medium heat, combine 5 cups of water and onion. Bring to a boil.
4. In a bowl, combine bagoong with ½ cup of the boiling water. In a fine mesh strainer, strain bagoong to extract juice and discard small fish particles from the bagoong.
5. Add the bagoong sauce into the pot. Lower heat to simmer.
6. Add the labong and continue to cook for about 7 to 10 minutes or until tender.
7. Add fried fish and cook for another 3 to 5 minutes to slightly soften and infuse flavor.
8. Add saluyot leaves and continue to cook for about 2 to 4 minutes or until just wilted.
9. Season with pepper to taste. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Prick 4 eggplants with a fork and roast over open flame until skin is charred. Allow to cool, peel the skin, and gently flatten with the back of a fork while keeping the stem intact.
2. In a bowl, beat 4 eggs with salt and pepper to taste until frothy.
3. Heat 1/4 cup cooking oil in a pan over medium heat.
4. Dip each eggplant in the egg mixture, coating well, and slide into the hot oil. Fry for 2-3 minutes per side or until golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. In a pot, heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 4 cloves minced garlic and 1 sliced onion until fragrant.\n2. Add 1 kg cubed unripe jackfruit and 1 cup water. Bring to a boil and cook for 10 minutes or until jackfruit is tender.\n3. Pour in 2 cans (800ml) coconut milk, add 1/2 cup sugar, and season with salt to taste. Simmer for 10 minutes or until sauce is thickened. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. In a pan, heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 2 cloves minced garlic and 1 chopped onion until fragrant.\n2. Add 2 julienned carrots, 1/2 shredded cabbage, 1/2 cup chopped green beans, and 1/2 cup bean sprouts. Stir in 2 tbsp soy sauce and cook for 5 minutes.\n3. Wrap the mixture in 20 lumpia wrappers and fry in hot oil until golden. Serve with sweet chili sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 chopped onion, and 2 chopped tomatoes.\n2. Add 200g ground pork and cook until browned.\n3. Add 500g sliced green beans and 3 tbsp soy sauce. Cook until tender. Season with salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 4 cloves minced garlic, 1 chopped onion, and 2 chopped tomatoes.\n2. Add 1 sliced bitter melon, 2 sliced eggplants, 10 okra, and 1/2 sliced squash. Stir in 3 tbsp bagoong alamang and cook until vegetables are tender.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 4 cloves minced garlic, 1 chopped onion, and 2 chopped tomatoes.\n2. Add 500g sliced eggplant and 1 cup water. Simmer until eggplant is tender.\n3. Stir in 2 beaten eggs and cook until eggs are set. Season with salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 4 cloves minced garlic, 1 chopped onion, and 2 chopped tomatoes.\n2. Add 500g cubed squash and 1/4 cup soy sauce, 1/4 cup vinegar. Simmer until squash is tender. Season with salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 chopped onion, and 2 chopped tomatoes.\n2. Add 2 cups malunggay leaves and cook until wilted. Season with salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp cooking oil and sauté 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 chopped onion, and 1 tbsp minced ginger.
2. Add 2 cups cubed kalabasa (squash) and 4 cups water. Bring to boil.
3. Simmer until squash is tender. Season with fish sauce.
4. Add 1 cup malunggay leaves and cook for 2 minutes.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil 6 cups water with tamarind soup mix or fresh tamarind.
2. Add 1 sliced radish and 2 sliced eggplants. Simmer for 5 minutes.
3. Add 1 bunch kangkong and 10 pieces sitaw (string beans). Cook until tender.
4. Season with fish sauce if needed.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Cook 1 cup rice in 6 cups water to make congee.
2. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger.
3. Add 1 cup carrots, 1 cup cabbage, and 1 cup malunggay leaves.
4. Add to congee and simmer until vegetables are tender.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Cook 200g udon noodles according to package.
2. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger.
3. Add 1 cup carrots, 1 cup cabbage, 1 cup broccoli, 1 bell pepper.
4. Add 2 cups vegetable broth and cooked noodles. Season with soy sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger.
2. Add 1 cup carrots, 1 cup cabbage, 1 cup cauliflower, 1 cup broccoli, 1 bell pepper.
3. Stir-fry until crisp-tender.
4. Add 2 cups vegetable broth and 3 tbsp soy sauce. Thicken with cornstarch slurry.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion.
2. Add 1 cup cabbage, 1 cup carrots, 200g tofu.
3. Season with soy sauce. Cool.
4. Wrap in lumpia wrappers and fry until golden.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Chop 2 tomatoes, 1 onion, 1 cucumber.\n2. Mix with juice of 5 calamansi.\n3. Season with bagoong alamang to taste.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Wash and chop lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, carrots, onions.\n2. Toss with juice of 6 calamansi and salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Roast 2 eggplants, 4 tomatoes, 2 onions over open flame until charred.\n2. Peel and mash with 3 cloves garlic.\n3. Season with salt and bagoong alamang.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.
2. Add 200g okra and 1 cup cubed kalabasa.
3. Add 2 cups water and simmer until tender.
4. Season with bagoong alamang.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 3 sayote, sliced. Cook until tender.\n3. Season with fish sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 4 cloves garlic, 1 onion.\n2. Add 1/4 cup soy sauce, 1/4 cup vinegar, 1 cup water. Boil.\n3. Add 2 cups malunggay leaves and 4 boiled eggs. Simmer.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 tbsp ginger.
2. Add 2 sayote, cubed, and 4 cups water. Bring to boil.
3. Simmer until sayote is tender. Add 1 cup malunggay leaves.
4. Season with fish sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil 6 cups water with onion and garlic.
2. Add 2 potatoes, 2 carrots, 1 cup green beans, 1 cup cabbage.
3. Simmer until vegetables are tender.
4. Season with salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Mix 1 cup vinegar, 1/2 cup sugar, 2 tbsp salt.\n2. Add 2 cups cabbage, 1 cup carrots, 1 cup cauliflower, 1 cup green beans.\n3. Let sit for 1 hour to pickle.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 4 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 2 potatoes, 2 carrots, 2 eggplants, 1 bell pepper.\n3. Add 1 cup tomato sauce and 1 cup water. Simmer until tender.

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 3 cucumbers, sliced. Cook for 3-5 minutes.\n3. Season with fish sauce.

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 1 bunch kangkong. Cook until wilted.\n3. Season with fish sauce.

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 2 eggplants and 5 labuyo peppers. Stir-fry until tender.\n3. Season with fish sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 4 cloves garlic, 1 onion.\n2. Add 2 potatoes, 2 carrots, 4 tomatoes.\n3. Add 1 cup tomato sauce and 1 cup water. Simmer until tender.

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 1 bunch pechay. Cook until wilted.\n3. Season with fish sauce.

recipes.strInstructions: 1. Heat 2 tbsp oil and sauté 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 2 tomatoes.\n2. Add 1/2 head cabbage, sliced. Cook until tender.\n3. Season with fish sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and peel the amaryl leaves and stems.
2. In a pot, boil water and add the amaryl.
3. Cook for 5-10 minutes until tender.
4. Season with salt and serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic and onion in butter.
2. Add moringa leaves and cook until wilted.
3. Pour in cream and broth.
4. Simmer for 10 minutes.
5. Season with salt and pepper.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Marinate vegetables in soy sauce, vinegar, and calamansi juice.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
3. Add marinated vegetables and cook until tender.
4. Season with pepper and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Cut water spinach into 2-inch pieces, blanch for 1 minute.
2. Heat oil in a wok, sauté 4 cloves garlic and 1 sliced onion until fragrant.
3. Add 2 tbsp vinegar and 3 tbsp soy sauce, simmer for 1 minute.
4. Add the blanched kaluway, 1 tsp black pepper, and mix thoroughly.
5. Cook on high heat for 2 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
6. Stir in 1 tsp cooking oil before serving hot with rice.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil 300g unripe papaya pieces in salted water for 3 minutes until tender.
2. Sauté 3 cloves minced garlic in 2 tbsp oil for 30 seconds.
3. Add 100g thinly sliced siniguelas fruit, stir for 1 minute.
4. Add the cooked papaya cubes, 1 tbsp vinegar, mix well.
5. Season with 2 tbsp fish sauce and black pepper.
6. Serve warm or at room temperature.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté 4 cloves crushed garlic in 3 tbsp oil until golden.
2. Add 150g bitter melon (ampalaya) pieces, stir for 2 minutes.
3. Add 100g okra (ladies fingers), 100g string beans, 150g eggplant chunks.
4. Pour in 3 tbsp fish sauce, ½ cup water, and 100g fresh shrimp.
5. Simmer for 8 minutes, add ½ cup fresh shiitake mushrooms.
6. Cook another 3 minutes until vegetables are tender, serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat 3 tbsp oil in a pan, sauté 5 cloves sliced garlic until light brown.
2. Add 1 diced onion and cook for 1 minute to soften.
3. Pour in 200g mussels (tahong), cook covered for 3 minutes until shells open.
4. Add 400g chopped pechay (bok choy), ½ tsp salt, ¼ tsp pepper.
5. Stir well and cook for 3 minutes until vegetables are tender.
6. Finish with 1 tbsp fish sauce and serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté 6 cloves minced garlic in 2 tbsp oil for 30 seconds.
2. Add 1 cup coconut milk, heat for 1 minute.
3. Stir in 30 taro leaves (malanga), 200g pumpkin cubes, 100g string beans.
4. Add 100g shrimp or small fish for protein, ½ tsp shrimp paste.
5. Simmer for 8 minutes until taro is tender, season with salt.
6. Cook another 2 minutes and garnish with whole red chili pepper.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Prepare the vegetables by washing and cutting into bite-sized pieces.
2. Heat oil in a pan or wok.
3. Sauté onions and garlic until fragrant.
4. Add the main vegetables and stir well.
5. Season with soy sauce, vinegar, and salt to taste.
6. Cook until vegetables are tender but still firm.
7. Serve hot with steamed rice.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

1. Rinse salted eggs under cold water.
2. Place in a pot and cover with water.
3. Boil for 12-15 minutes until fully cooked.
4. Let cool then peel.
5. Slice in half and arrange on a plate.
6. Garnish with tomatoes and onions.
7. Drizzle with a little vinegar if desired.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil pork belly until tender, then slice thinly.
2. Fry tofu until golden brown, then cube.
3. Mix soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, onion, and chili for the dipping sauce.
4. Arrange pork and tofu on a plate.
5. Pour sauce over or serve on the side.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Clean and score tilapia on both sides.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
4. Fry tilapia for 5-7 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
5. Drain on paper towels.
6. Serve with sawsawan (vinegar-garlic dip).
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil mung beans until soft.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Add tinapa flakes and stir.
4. Pour in the boiled mung beans with broth.
5. Add ampalaya leaves or malunggay.
6. Season with fish sauce.
7. Simmer for 5 minutes and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
2. Add ground pork and cook until brown.
3. Pour in canned pork and beans.
4. Add tomato sauce and sugar.
5. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Simmer for 10 minutes until thick.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Beat eggs with salt and pepper.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
3. Add diced tomatoes and cook until soft.
4. Pour in beaten eggs.
5. Stir gently until eggs are just set.
6. Season to taste and serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Clean and butterfly the bangus.
2. Season with salt, garlic powder, and pepper.
3. Squeeze calamansi over the fish.
4. Marinate for 15 minutes.
5. Heat oil and fry bangus until golden and crispy on both sides.
6. Drain and serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Marinate chicken liver in soy sauce, vinegar, and garlic for 20 minutes.
2. Cook in a pan over medium heat with the marinade.
3. Bring to a boil then simmer until liquid reduces.
4. Add bay leaves and peppercorns.
5. Cook until liver is done and sauce thickens.
6. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strMeal: Tuyo at Itlog (Dried Fish and Egg)
recipes.strInstructions:
1. Fry tuyo in a little oil until crispy.
2. In the same pan, fry eggs sunny side up.
3. Arrange tuyo and eggs on a plate.
4. Serve with sliced tomatoes and hot rice.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
2. Add ground pork and cook until brown.
3. Add diced potatoes and carrots.
4. Season with soy sauce, fish sauce, and pepper.
5. Pour in a little water and simmer until vegetables are soft.
6. Add green peas and cook for 2 more minutes.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes in oil.
2. Add canned sardines and mash slightly.
3. Stir and cook for 3 minutes.
4. Add green peppers if desired.
5. Season with pepper and fish sauce.
6. Serve hot with rice.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Open and drain canned tuna.
2. Mix tuna with mayonnaise.
3. Add diced celery or carrots for crunch.
4. Season with salt, pepper, and calamansi juice.
5. Chill briefly before serving.
6. Serve as a side or sandwich filling.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Clean squid and slice into rings.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add squid and cook for 2 minutes.
4. Pour in soy sauce and vinegar.
5. Add bay leaves and peppercorns.
6. Simmer until squid is cooked and sauce reduces.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
2. Marinate in soy sauce and calamansi for 30 minutes.
3. Heat oil in a pan.
4. Fry chicken on medium heat, 8-10 minutes per side.
5. Drain excess oil.
6. Serve with vinegar dip.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Place longganisa in a pan with 1/2 cup water.
2. Cook over medium heat until water evaporates.
3. Continue cooking in its own oil until browned and cooked through.
4. Serve with garlic fried rice and egg.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic and onion.
2. Add bagoong and cook for 1 minute.
3. Add pork and cook until browned.
4. Add squash and eggplant, stir.
5. Add ampalaya, okra, and long beans.
6. Pour in a little water and steam until vegetables are tender.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Marinate butterflied bangus in vinegar, garlic, and salt overnight.
2. Pat dry before cooking.
3. Heat oil and fry bangus bone-side down first.
4. Cook until golden and crispy, about 5 minutes per side.
5. Serve with garlic fried rice and tomatoes.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice beef thinly against the grain.
2. Marinate in soy sauce, calamansi, garlic, and sugar overnight.
3. Pan-fry marinated beef in a little oil over medium heat.
4. Cook until slightly caramelized, about 3-4 minutes per side.
5. Serve with garlic rice and egg.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a pan over low-medium heat.
2. Add raw peanuts and fry slowly, stirring continuously.
3. Add garlic cloves and continue frying.
4. When peanuts turn golden, add salt.
5. Drain on paper towels.
6. Serve as a side or snack.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Grill or broil eggplants until skin is charred.
2. Peel and flatten the eggplant.
3. Beat eggs and season with salt and pepper.
4. Dip flattened eggplant in beaten egg.
5. Pan-fry in oil until egg sets and both sides are golden.
6. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil chicken breast until tender.
2. Shred or chop finely.
3. Sauté onions and chili peppers.
4. Add chopped chicken and season with soy sauce, calamansi, and mayonnaise.
5. Serve sizzling with egg on top.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic and onion.
2. Add pork pieces and cook until browned.
3. Pour in pork blood and stir well.
4. Add vinegar and long green peppers.
5. Season with fish sauce and sugar.
6. Simmer until sauce thickens.
7. Serve with puto.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Steam mussels until shells open, remove meat.
2. Dredge mussel meat in flour seasoned with salt and pepper.
3. Dip in beaten egg.
4. Fry in hot oil until golden brown.
5. Drain and serve with vinegar dip.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Place eggs in a pot of cold water.
2. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
3. Cook for 10-12 minutes for hard-boiled.
4. Transfer to cold water to stop cooking.
5. Peel and slice in half.
6. Drizzle with soy sauce and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil pork skin with salt until tender.
2. Drain and dry under the sun or in oven at low temp.
3. Deep fry in very hot oil until puffed and crispy.
4. Drain on paper towels.
5. Season with salt and serve with vinegar.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Clean bangus and stuff with tomatoes, onions, and ginger.
2. Season inside and out with salt.
3. Wrap in foil or banana leaf.
4. Grill over medium coals for 20-25 minutes, turning once.
5. Serve with soy-vinegar dip.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Press tofu to remove excess water.
2. Slice into thick pieces.
3. Season with soy sauce, garlic, and pepper.
4. Pan-fry until golden on both sides.
5. Make sauce with soy sauce, calamansi, and onion rings.
6. Pour sauce over tofu and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strMeal: Tahong sa Gata (Mussels in Coconut Milk)
recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic, ginger, and onion.
2. Add mussels and stir.
3. Pour in coconut milk.
4. Add chili peppers and season with fish sauce.
5. Simmer until mussels open and sauce thickens slightly.
6. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil mung beans until soft.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Add pork or shrimp and cook.
4. Add the boiled mung beans.
5. Season with fish sauce.
6. Add malunggay or ampalaya leaves.
7. Simmer for 5 minutes.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Season bangus belly with salt and pepper.
2. Marinate in soy sauce and calamansi for 15 minutes.
3. Coat lightly in flour.
4. Pan-fry in hot oil until golden and crispy.
5. Serve with spiced vinegar.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Husk and clean the corn.
2. Place in a pot and cover with water.
3. Add salt and butter if desired.
4. Boil for 15-20 minutes until tender.
5. Serve hot with butter and salt.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Grill eggplants over open flame until charred.
2. Peel and shred the flesh.
3. Mix with diced tomatoes and onions.
4. Dress with fish sauce, vinegar, and calamansi.
5. Top with salted egg if desired.
6. Serve cold or at room temperature.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and cut broccoli into florets.
2. Steam for 5-7 minutes until tender-crisp.
3. In a pan, heat oyster sauce with a little water.
4. Add sesame oil and garlic.
5. Pour sauce over steamed broccoli.
6. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Shred cabbage into thin strips.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
3. Add cabbage and stir-fry over high heat.
4. Add carrots and bell pepper if desired.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and soy sauce.
6. Cook until cabbage is wilted but still crisp.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and slice sweet potato into rounds.
2. Heat oil in a deep pan.
3. Add brown sugar to the oil and let melt.
4. Add sweet potato slices.
5. Fry until cooked and caramelized.
6. Skewer and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and scrub sweet potatoes.
2. Place in a pot and cover with water.
3. Boil for 20-25 minutes until tender.
4. Let cool slightly and peel.
5. Serve as is or with butter.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice ampalaya thinly and soak in salted water to reduce bitterness.
2. Squeeze and drain.
3. Sauté garlic and onion.
4. Add ampalaya and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
5. Push to the side, scramble eggs in the center.
6. Mix together and season with salt and pepper.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and drain bean sprouts.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add tofu or shrimp for protein.
4. Add bean sprouts and stir-fry quickly over high heat.
5. Season with soy sauce, pepper, and fish sauce.
6. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice tomatoes and onions.
2. Arrange on a plate.
3. Drizzle with fish sauce and calamansi.
4. Add bagoong or salted egg if desired.
5. Toss gently and serve fresh.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and cube squash.
2. Cut long beans into 2-inch pieces.
3. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
4. Add squash and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Add long beans and stir.
6. Pour a little water, cover, and steam until tender.
7. Season with fish sauce.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and trim pechay.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add shrimp or ground pork.
4. Add pechay and stir-fry quickly.
5. Season with oyster sauce, salt, and pepper.
6. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Grate unripe papaya and squeeze out liquid.
2. Salt and let stand for 30 minutes, then squeeze dry.
3. Julienne carrots and bell peppers.
4. Boil vinegar and sugar until dissolved.
5. Add garlic, ginger, and spices.
6. Combine vegetables and pour hot brine over.
7. Cool and store in jars.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic, onion, and shrimp paste.
2. Add squash cubes and stir.
3. Pour in coconut milk.
4. Simmer until squash is tender.
5. Add long beans and chili.
6. Season with fish sauce.
7. Simmer until sauce thickens.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Grill or fry eggplants until cooked.
2. Slice and arrange on a plate.
3. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
4. Add bagoong and cook for 2 minutes.
5. Add vinegar and sugar.
6. Pour bagoong sauce over eggplants.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Bring water to a boil and add bagoong.
2. Add squash and cook for 3 minutes.
3. Add eggplant, string beans, and bitter melon.
4. Add grilled or fried fish.
5. Season with more bagoong to taste.
6. Simmer until all vegetables are cooked.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil corn cobs in water until tender.
2. Remove corn kernels from cob.
3. Sauté garlic and onion.
4. Add mixed vegetables and corn.
5. Pour in broth.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Simmer for 10 minutes.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil unripe jackfruit until slightly tender.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and bagoong.
3. Add the jackfruit and stir.
4. Pour in coconut milk.
5. Add long beans and chili.
6. Simmer until sauce thickens.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Strip malunggay leaves from stems.
2. Sauté garlic and onion in oil.
3. Add shrimp or ground pork.
4. Add malunggay leaves.
5. Season with salt and fish sauce.
6. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash kangkong and cut into pieces.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add shrimp paste (bagoong) and cook 1 minute.
4. Add kangkong and stir-fry over high heat.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and soy sauce.
6. Cook until just wilted and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and slice carrots and sayote.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add carrots first and cook for 2 minutes.
4. Add sayote and stir.
5. Season with oyster sauce, salt, and pepper.
6. Add a splash of water and cover.
7. Cook until vegetables are tender-crisp.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and trim okra.
2. Boil in salted water for 5 minutes.
3. Drain and arrange on a plate.
4. Mix bagoong with calamansi and chili.
5. Serve okra with bagoong dip on the side.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and slice radish thinly.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add ground pork or shrimp.
4. Add radish slices and stir.
5. Season with fish sauce and pepper.
6. Add water and simmer until radish is tender.
7. Garnish with green onions.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and trim winged beans.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add shrimp or pork.
4. Add sigarilyas and stir-fry.
5. Season with fish sauce and pepper.
6. Cook for 5-7 minutes until tender.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Shred young coconut meat.
2. Mix with nata de coco and kaong.
3. Add fruit cocktail and drain liquid.
4. Mix in condensed milk and all-purpose cream.
5. Toss gently to combine.
6. Chill for at least 1 hour.
7. Serve cold.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strMeal: Gising-Gising (Spicy Green Beans in Coconut Milk)
recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice green beans thinly.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and chili peppers.
3. Add ground pork and cook.
4. Add shrimp paste and cook 1 minute.
5. Add green beans and stir.
6. Pour in coconut milk and simmer.
7. Season and cook until beans are tender.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Bring water to a boil.
2. Add bagoong and simmer for 2 minutes.
3. Add squash and cook until half-done.
4. Add eggplant, string beans, and other vegetables.
5. Add malunggay leaves and stir.
6. Simmer until all vegetables are cooked.
7. Adjust seasoning with bagoong.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Clean and slice mushrooms.
2. Heat butter or oil in a pan.
3. Add garlic and sauté until fragrant.
4. Add mushrooms and cook over medium-high heat.
5. Season with soy sauce, salt, and pepper.
6. Cook until mushrooms are golden.
7. Garnish with parsley and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strMeal: Pinais na Dalag (Mudfish in Banana Leaf)
recipes.strInstructions:
1. Clean and season fish with salt.
2. Lay banana leaf and top with fish.
3. Add ginger, lemongrass, tomatoes, and onions.
4. Wrap tightly.
5. Steam for 25-30 minutes.
6. Serve still wrapped, opening at the table.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash fresh peanuts with shells.
2. Place in a pot with water and salt.
3. Bring to a boil and simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
4. Taste for saltiness and tenderness.
5. Drain and serve warm.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and trim pechay.
2. Steam for 3-5 minutes until wilted.
3. Heat oyster sauce with garlic and sesame oil.
4. Pour sauce over steamed pechay.
5. Garnish with fried garlic chips.
6. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Fry tofu until golden, then cube.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add mixed vegetables.
4. Add fried tofu.
5. Season with soy sauce and oyster sauce.
6. Add a splash of water.
7. Simmer until vegetables are cooked.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and julienne green mangoes.
2. Mix with diced red onion.
3. Dress with fish sauce, vinegar, and sugar.
4. Toss with dried shrimp (hibe) if desired.
5. Add chili for heat.
6. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and cut kangkong into sections.
2. Heat oil in a wok over high heat.
3. Add garlic and stir for 30 seconds.
4. Add kangkong and toss quickly.
5. Season with oyster sauce and pepper.
6. Cook for just 1-2 minutes until wilted.
7. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice banana blossom thinly.
2. Soak in salted water with calamansi to prevent browning.
3. Squeeze dry.
4. Mix with coconut milk, fish sauce, vinegar, and onion.
5. Add chili and ginger.
6. Let marinate 15 minutes.
7. Serve cold.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Choose ripe saba bananas.
2. Do not peel completely — leave some skin on.
3. Place in boiling water.
4. Cook for 10-15 minutes until soft.
5. Remove and serve warm.
6. Can be served with muscovado sugar.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil mung beans until soft.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Add boiled mung beans.
4. Add fresh spinach and stir.
5. Season with fish sauce and pepper.
6. Simmer 3 minutes and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté ginger, garlic, and onion.
2. Add chicken pieces and cook until golden.
3. Pour in fish sauce and broth.
4. Add green papaya cubes.
5. Simmer until papaya is tender.
6. Add malunggay leaves and chili leaves.
7. Season and serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil and peel salted eggs, slice in quarters.
2. Slice tomatoes and red onion.
3. Arrange on a plate.
4. Drizzle with a light vinaigrette of vinegar and a tiny bit of oil.
5. Top with fried garlic.
6. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and cut upo into strips or cubes.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Add shrimp or pork and cook.
4. Add upo and stir.
5. Season with fish sauce.
6. Add water and simmer until upo is translucent.
7. Serve hot.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash and blanch pako ferns briefly.
2. Drain and cool.
3. Mix with sliced tomatoes and onions.
4. Dress with fish sauce, vinegar, and calamansi.
5. Top with salted egg.
6. Toss and serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Combine glutinous rice flour and water to form a soft dough.
2. Shape into flat oval discs.
3. Drop in boiling water — they float when cooked.
4. Remove and drain.
5. Roll in grated coconut.
6. Sprinkle with sugar and sesame seeds.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil saba bananas until cooked, slice.
2. Place crushed ice in a glass.
3. Add sliced bananas.
4. Pour evaporated milk over.
5. Add sugar syrup to taste.
6. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice ampalaya and soak in salted water.
2. Squeeze to remove excess bitterness.
3. Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
4. Add ground pork and cook.
5. Add ampalaya and stir-fry.
6. Season with fish sauce and pepper.
7. Serve with rice.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash kamote tops and discard tough stems.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add bagoong or shrimp.
4. Add kamote leaves and stir-fry.
5. Season with fish sauce.
6. Cook 2-3 minutes and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and cube squash.
2. Steam for 10-12 minutes until tender.
3. Mix coconut cream with fish sauce, garlic, and calamansi.
4. Pour dressing over steamed squash.
5. Top with toasted coconut flakes.
6. Serve warm.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Roast bell peppers over flame or in oven.
2. Peel and slice into strips.
3. Mix with olive oil, garlic, and vinegar.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Add capers or olives if desired.
6. Let marinate 15 minutes and serve.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Boil guava until soft, mash and strain for broth.
2. Add pork or shrimp to the broth.
3. Add squash and cook for 5 minutes.
4. Add eggplant, okra, and string beans.
5. Season with fish sauce.
6. Add malunggay leaves.
7. Simmer until done.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strMeal: Ginisang Camote Tops with Salted Egg
recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash sweet potato leaves.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add sweet potato leaves.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Arrange on a plate.
6. Top with sliced salted egg.
7. Drizzle with vinegar dressing.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté garlic, onion, and ginger.
2. Add bagoong and cook 1 minute.
3. Add harder vegetables first (squash, eggplant).
4. Pour in coconut milk.
5. Add leafy vegetables and chili.
6. Season with fish sauce.
7. Simmer until all done.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice cucumber into rounds or half-moons.
2. Boil salted eggs, peel, and slice.
3. Arrange cucumber and egg on a plate.
4. Mix cane vinegar, sugar, and chili for dressing.
5. Pour over salad.
6. Top with fried garlic.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strMeal: Ginataang Mais (Sweet Corn in Coconut Milk)
recipes.strInstructions:
1. Combine coconut milk and water in a pot.
2. Add corn kernels and glutinous rice.
3. Stir constantly over medium heat.
4. Add sugar and salt.
5. Cook until rice is soft and mixture thickens.
6. Serve warm or chilled.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel sweet potatoes and slice into rounds.
2. Heat oil in a deep pan.
3. Fry sweet potato slices until golden and cooked through.
4. Drain on paper towels.
5. Sprinkle with a little salt or sugar.
6. Serve warm.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Wash spinach leaves.
2. Heat oil in a pan.
3. Add plenty of garlic and cook until golden.
4. Add spinach and toss quickly.
5. Season with salt and a splash of soy sauce.
6. Cook just until wilted.
7. Serve immediately.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Sauté pork belly cubes until browned.
2. Add garlic, onion, and tomatoes.
3. Add squash and eggplant.
4. Add okra and string beans.
5. Pour pork broth over vegetables.
6. Season with fish sauce.
7. Cover and simmer until all cooked.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Slice cucumbers and carrots thinly.
2. Salt and squeeze dry.
3. Make brine with vinegar, sugar, and salt.
4. Add garlic and peppercorns.
5. Pour over cucumber and carrot.
6. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes.
7. Serve as a side to grilled dishes.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Peel and slice sayote.
2. Sauté garlic and onion.
3. Add chicken or pork.
4. Add sayote and stir.
5. Pour in broth.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Simmer until sayote is tender.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included

recipes.strInstructions:
1. Prepare the vegetables by washing and cutting into bite-sized pieces.
2. Heat oil in a pan or wok.
3. Sauté onions and garlic until fragrant.
4. Add the main vegetables and stir well.
5. Season with soy sauce, vinegar, and salt to taste.
6. Cook until vegetables are tender but still firm.
7. Serve hot with steamed rice.
sources: No labor or overhead costs included
